name: Features Check List
description: Editorial 'everything you get' check list framed as a long-form value page rather than a sidebar widget. An optional 4-up project meta strip (plan / window / verified-by / lead) anchors the section above the list; rows render with one of three check styles (filled, outline, minimal), can carry an inline-bolded keyword and an optional clarifying note line, and can be flat or grouped under small section headers with their own description copy. Below the list, an optional methodology footnote, pull-quote, dark outcome metrics band and closing CTA let the section carry an entire plan-detail or scope-of-work page on its own.

descriptions.descriptive: Two-column list inside a 6xl container, with each row separated by a 1px hairline (no card boxes) and anchored by a stylized check icon — solid filled circle, outlined ring, or bare glyph — that pulses into view with a spring-driven scale animation on first scroll. An inline keyword can be bolded inside each line for scan-anchors and an optional note line under the row carries the methodology nuance the way ComparisonSplit's per-row notes do. Above the list, an optional 4-up project meta strip frames the section as a single engagement; immediately above the rows, a mono-caps summary line counts items (or groups + items) so long lists read as a deliberate manifest. Group variants insert a base-content/15 hairline plus an optional one-line group description, breaking longer lists into named clusters. Below the list, an optional mono-caps footnote (methodology / fine print), a serif pull-quote on a primary-tinted left rule, a dark base-content metrics band with mono numerals at text-5xl on desktop, and a closing CTA close the case. Reduced-motion users get a static fallback; everyone else gets a 0.08s staggered fade-up reveal on the header and meta strip plus the per-icon spring pulse.
descriptions.usage: Use whenever the value of a plan, package or scope of work benefits from a flat, scannable manifest with editorial framing — drop next to a pricing component (tier cards, monthly/annual toggle, single price) to reinforce 'yes, this is included' for every line; use as a what's-in-the-plan list under a hero; use as a deliverables block in a consulting proposal page. Use the grouped variant when the list crosses twelve items and benefits from named buckets like 'Strategy', 'Build', 'Support'. The optional meta strip, per-row notes, footnote, pull-quote, metrics band and CTA let the section stand in for a full scope page. Place after a hero or below a stats panel; never duplicate it on the same page with a comparison table covering the same axis.
descriptions.audienceFit: Fits SaaS subscription pages, productised consulting, membership programs, ecommerce bundle pages, enterprise scope-of-work, and any audience that scans a check list before reading prose. The minimal density, mono-caps eyebrows and quiet motion keep it credible for B2B and enterprise buyers; the friendly check pulse and the optional pull-quote add the small reward indie and SMB readers respond to.
